meta.question: What is the origin of SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19)?

factors.label: Epidemiological proximity of the earliest cases to the market
factors.rationale: Spatial clustering of early cases and positive samples at the market is the load-bearing zoonotic argument.

provenance.quote: We show the earliest known COVID-19 cases from December 2019, including those without reported direct links, were geographically centered on this market.
The HSM was a site for amplification, which allowed the virus to spread further, but it is not possible to determine if the first infections occurred at the HSM .

factors.label: Ascertainment / reporting bias in early Wuhan case data
factors.rationale: Argues the market centering is not merely a surveillance artifact.

provenance.quote: The wet market connection, the major point of similarity with the SARS1 and MERS epidemics, was soon broken: Chinese researchers found earlier cases in Wuhan with no link to the wet market.
We show the earliest known COVID-19 cases from December 2019, including those without reported direct links, were geographically centered on this market.

Phylogenetic analysis of these sequences in the context of carefully annotated existing data further supports the idea that the Huanan Seafood Market sequences are not fully representative of the viruses in Wuhan early in the epidemic.
I also demonstrate clear evidence of bias among the earliest available sequences, and explain why the root of the SARS-CoV-2 tree cannot be conclusively resolved with the current data.

factors.label: Number of introductions (single seeding vs multiple spillovers)
factors.rationale: Two independent introductions are hard to reconcile with a single lab escape.

provenance.quote: Phylodynamic rooting methods, coupled with epidemic simulations, reveal that these lineages were the result of at least two separate cross-species transmission events into humans.

factors.label: Furin cleavage site as evidence of laboratory engineering
factors.rationale: Reads the genome features as consistent with natural evolution.

provenance.quote: The acquisition of both the polybasic cleavage site and predicted O-linked glycans also argues against culture-based scenarios.
The furin cleavage site in the spike protein of SARS-CoV-2 confers to the virus the ability to cross species and tissue barriers, but was previously unseen in other SARS-like CoVs.
We also noted, correctly, that placing the insertion out of frame would be “an unusual and needlessly complex feat of genetic engineering.”

To evaluate hypothesis #4, of a deliberate manipulation of the virus in a laboratory and subsequent biosafety breach, SAGO analysed the genome structure of the virus and publications and reports addressing the likelihood of manipulation through reverse-genetics but did not find scientific evidence supporting this hypothesis over evidence that these mutations and recombination events also occur in coronaviruses in nature .

factors.label: Prior probability of a research-related accident in Wuhan
factors.rationale: Documented intent to engineer a furin site raises the lab prior.

provenance.quote: Specifically, the scientists sought to insert furin cleavage sites at the S1/S2 junction of the spike protein; to assemble synthetic viruses in six segments; to identify coronaviruses up to 25 percent different from SARS; and to select for receptor binding domains adept at infecting human receptors.
It is a 370 nucleotide virus fragment collected from the Mojiang mine in 2013 by the lab of Zeng-li Shi at the WIV ( Ge et al., 2016 ).
Information and evidence is also lacking to assess the possibility of a laboratory origin - either the evidence is not available or has not been provided to the scientific community .

factors.label: Reliability of Bayesian priors and likelihood estimates
factors.rationale: The whole conclusion is driven by chosen priors/likelihoods over shared evidence.

provenance.quote: The mistakes were heavily skewed toward zoonosis, since our methodology involves steelmanning and maximizing the likelihoods of both hypotheses, while Miller used figures heavily biased toward zoonosis, in some cases using extreme estimates that are impossible to reach in a robust probabilistic analysis, as we explain below.

Methods The standard logical procedure to calculate the odds, P(LL)/P(ZW), is to combine some rough prior sense of the odds with judgments of how consistent new pieces of evidence are with the LL and ZW hypotheses.

factors.label: Existence of a close natural bat-virus ancestor
factors.rationale: A near relative in bats supports a natural reservoir.

provenance.quote: Furthermore, we show that 2019-nCoV is 96% identical at the whole-genome level to a bat coronavirus.

As expected from the high affinity for hACE2 of the S ectodomain of BANAL-236, pseudoviruses expressing it were able to efficiently enter human cells expressing endogenous hACE2 using an ACE2-dependent pathway.

position.quote: While there is insufficient evidence to define upstream events, and exact circumstances remain obscure, our analyses indicate that the emergence of SARS-CoV-2 occurred via the live wildlife trade in China, and show that the Huanan market was the epicenter of the COVID-19 pandemic.
